summary_strategy: Position Conner as a pragmatic builder-operator who ships deployable security controls and automation — emphasizing measurable risk reduction, operational friction reduction, and secure defaults in cloud-native environments

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, Bash, PowerShell (for automation), TypeScript/JavaScript (for web tooling), and SQL (for data work); keep all 11 languages intact
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to highlight vulnerability management, SIEM/EDR/XDR, endpoint management, and patch orchestration first; add CrowdStrike Falcon/RTR, Defender XDR, Zeek from inventory for credibility
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Rename to 'AI/ML for SecOps' only if strictly required — but per instructions, retain exact name; reorder to lead with RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, NLP for security context; add embeddings-based retrieval and AI governance from inventory
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to highlight Flask, FastAPI (for internal tooling), Docker, React.js; add MCP/FastMCP earlier as it aligns with Loancrate’s tooling style
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, secrets management; add GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Terraform, Ansible from inventory where relevant
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, MySQL, DynamoDB (cloud-native), vector databases; keep all 10 databases intact

experience_focus.company: University of California, Office of the President
experience_focus.must_highlight: Coraline (multi-source asset reconciliation), AI/ML security chatbot (RAG + SecOps), vulnerability response automation for macOS/Windows
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No invented metrics (e.g., avoid 'X% reduction' unless explicitly stated), no overclaiming AI capabilities beyond RAG/NLP

experience_focus.must_highlight: Azure hybrid tenant automation, BitLocker enforcement via PowerShell, KnowBe4 phishing analysis tooling
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 200 hours saved annually, 500 hours reduced workload
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No vague claims like 'improved security posture' without evidence; avoid conflating tools (e.g., don’t say 'used CrowdStrike for BitLocker' — it’s separate tooling)

experience_focus.must_highlight: CI/CD pipeline automation, Selenium-based accessibility testing (WCAG/ADA), cross-browser reliability
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues (explicitly stated)
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No invented client names or project scopes; avoid implying fintech compliance work unless tied to actual deliverables

risk_controls: Do not invent metrics — only retain numbers explicitly present in baseline resume
Never claim ownership of SOC 2, penetration testing, or vendor risk programs unless directly evidenced; instead say 'supported evidence collection' or 'aligned with controls'
Avoid FUD language — replace phrases like 'mitigated critical risk' with 'enabled compliance', 'reduced manual effort', or 'operationalized controls'
Do not merge or reassign responsibilities across roles — keep UC (security engineer), Great Wolf (support engineer), Simple.biz (freelance dev) distinct in framing
